mission.title: istio: Istio Ambient mode - Routing egress traffic to wildcard destinations
mission.description: Istio Ambient mode - Routing egress traffic to wildcard destinations. Requested by 15+ users.

steps.title: Check current istio deployment
steps.description:
Verify your istio version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
helm list -n istio-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working istio installation.

steps.title: Review istio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n istio-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get configmap -n istio-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio
```
### Is this the right place to submit this?

- [X] This is not a security vulnerability or a crashing bug
- [X] This is not a question about how to use Istio

### Bug Description

Hi Team,

We are planing to use istio ambient mode to route egress

steps.title: Apply the fix for Istio Ambient mode - Routing egress traffic to wildcard…
steps.description:
We have similar use cases using 3rd party tools where we don't have a list of hostnames they need access to. For instance https://external-secrets.io/latest/ it took looking through various logs to find the ip addresses and hostnames that needed to be created as service entries to allow external
```yaml
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1
kind: ServiceEntry
metadata:
  name: wildcard
  namespace: common-infrastructure
spec:
  hosts:
    - "*.com"
    - "*.net"
    - "*.org"
    - "*.io"
  ports:
  - number: 80
    name: http
    protocol: HTTP
  resolution: DNS
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get events -n istio-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Istio Ambient mode - Routing egress traffic to wildcard…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: We have similar use cases using 3rd party tools where we don't have a list of hostnames they need access to. For instance https://external-secrets.io/latest/ it took looking through various logs to find the ip addresses and hostnames that needed to be created as service entries to allow external secrets to work correctly.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1
kind: ServiceEntry
metadata:
  name: wildcard
  namespace: common-infrastructure
spec:
  hosts:
    - "*.com"
    - "*.net"
    - "*.org"
    - "*.io"
  ports:
  - number: 80
    name: http
    protocol: HTTP
  resolution: DNS

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
